request.intent: Looking for feel-good content, family-friendly, uplifting moments, under 20 min per clip

response.viewer_intent_interpretation: Looking for feel-good content, family-friendly, uplifting moments, under 20 min per clip

recommended_rail.title: Sous chefs and prize revealed
recommended_rail.rationale: Feel-Good Family · Sous chefs and prize revealed — Ramsay introduces Blue Team sous chef James Avery and Red Team sous chef Michelle Tribble, then announces the prize: the winner will become head chef at Hell's Kitchen at Foxwoods Resort Casino
recommended_rail.audience_alignment_reasoning: For a Feel-Good Family viewer, "Sous chefs and prize revealed" in Ep 1 delivers warmth and resolution without harsh conflict — ideal for co-viewing families. In this window, Gordon Ramsay, Brandon, Hannah drive the beat: Ramsay introduces Blue Team sous chef James Avery and Red Team sous chef Michelle Tribble, then announces the prize: the winner will become head chef at Hell's Kitchen at Foxwoods Resort Casino.  The chefs react with excitement because the reward is a real executive position, not just bragging rights. That aligns with your looking-for signals (feel-good content, family-friendly, uplifting moments) and keeps your configured character spotlight visible, while steering clear of violence, harsh language, elimination drama.
recommended_rail.clip_description: Ramsay introduces Blue Team sous chef James Avery and Red Team sous chef Michelle Tribble, then announces the prize: the winner will become head chef at Hell's Kitchen at Foxwoods Resort Casino.  The chefs react with excitement because the reward is a real executive position, not just bragging rights.
recommended_rail.sub_clip_focus: Sous chefs and prize revealed — Ramsay introduces Blue Team sous chef James Avery and Red Team sous chef Michelle Tribble, then announces the prize: the winner will become head chef at Hell's Kitchen at Foxwoods Resort Casino

recommended_rail.title: Red celebrates while Blue absorbs the loss
recommended_rail.rationale: Feel-Good Family · Red celebrates while Blue absorbs the loss — The Red Team celebrates its reward, first discovering the cookware surprise and then enjoying the bowling outing
recommended_rail.audience_alignment_reasoning: For a Feel-Good Family viewer, "Red celebrates while Blue absorbs the loss" in Ep 5 delivers warmth and resolution without harsh conflict — ideal for co-viewing families. In this window, Amanda, Ann Marie drive the beat: The Red Team celebrates its reward, first discovering the cookware surprise and then enjoying the bowling outing.  In parallel, the Blue Team grinds meat by hand while Kyle privately spirals over costing them the challenge. That aligns with your looking-for signals (feel-good content, family-friendly, uplifting moments) and keeps your configured character spotlight visible, while steering clear of violence, harsh language, elimination drama.
recommended_rail.clip_label: Red celebrates while Blue absorbs the loss
recommended_rail.clip_description: The Red Team celebrates its reward, first discovering the cookware surprise and then enjoying the bowling outing.  In parallel, the Blue Team grinds meat by hand while Kyle privately spirals over costing them the challenge.
recommended_rail.sub_clip_focus: Red celebrates while Blue absorbs the loss — The Red Team celebrates its reward, first discovering the cookware surprise and then enjoying the bowling outing

recommended_rail.title: Momentum swings in the middle rounds
recommended_rail.rationale: Feel-Good Family · Momentum swings in the middle rounds — The middle judging round is all about instability
recommended_rail.audience_alignment_reasoning: For a Feel-Good Family viewer, "Momentum swings in the middle rounds" in Ep 1 delivers warmth and resolution without harsh conflict — ideal for co-viewing families. In this window, Gordon Ramsay, Kyle, Brittany drive the beat: The middle judging round is all about instability.  Kyle earns praise for beautifully cooked fish, Brittany successfully recovers enough to score a 4, Amber stumbles with a 3, and Corbin capitalizes with a 4 that briefly puts the Blue Team ahead. That aligns with your looking-for signals (feel-good content, family-friendly, uplifting moments) and keeps your configured character spotlight visible, while steering clear of violence, harsh language, elimination drama.
recommended_rail.clip_label: Momentum swings in the middle rounds
recommended_rail.clip_description: The middle judging round is all about instability.  Kyle earns praise for beautifully cooked fish, Brittany successfully recovers enough to score a 4, Amber stumbles with a 3, and Corbin capitalizes with a 4 that briefly puts the Blue Team ahead.
recommended_rail.sub_clip_focus: Momentum swings in the middle rounds — The middle judging round is all about instability

recommended_rail.title: Reward bonds the women; punishment divides the men
recommended_rail.rationale: Feel-Good Family · Reward bonds the women; punishment divides the men — After the loss, Ramsay sends the Red Team to a reward dinner with him, where their shared celebration builds immediate camaraderie and 'girl power' energy
recommended_rail.audience_alignment_reasoning: For a Feel-Good Family viewer, "Reward bonds the women; punishment divides the men" in Ep 1 delivers warmth and resolution without harsh conflict — ideal for co-viewing families. In this window, Gordon Ramsay, Brittany, Ann Marie drive the beat: After the loss, Ramsay sends the Red Team to a reward dinner with him, where their shared celebration builds immediate camaraderie and 'girl power' energy.  The Blue Team, meanwhile, is first ordered to clean the trashed kitchen and then unload a delivery truck; during the punishment, Uri's choice to handle inventory rather than lift boxes frustrates teammates and sparks the first meaningful internal resentment on the men's side. That aligns with your looking-for signals (feel-good content, family-friendly, uplifting moments) and keeps your configured character spotlight visible, while steering clear of violence, harsh language, elimination drama.
recommended_rail.clip_label: Reward bonds the women; punishment divides the men
recommended_rail.clip_description: After the loss, Ramsay sends the Red Team to a reward dinner with him, where their shared celebration builds immediate camaraderie and 'girl power' energy.  The Blue Team, meanwhile, is first ordered to clean the trashed kitchen and then unload a delivery truck; during the punishment, Uri's choice to handle inventory rather than lift boxes frustrates teammates and sparks the first meaningful internal resentment on the men's side.
recommended_rail.sub_clip_focus: Reward bonds the women; punishment divides the men — After the loss, Ramsay sends the Red Team to a reward dinner with him, where their shared celebration builds immediate camaraderie and 'girl power' energy

recommended_rail.title: Both kitchens recover and find rhythm
recommended_rail.rationale: Feel-Good Family · Both kitchens recover and find rhythm — After the rough opening, the kitchens settle
recommended_rail.audience_alignment_reasoning: For a Feel-Good Family viewer, "Both kitchens recover and find rhythm" in Ep 5 delivers warmth and resolution without harsh conflict — ideal for co-viewing families. In this window, Gordon Ramsay, Egypt, Anthony drive the beat: After the rough opening, the kitchens settle.  Egypt regains confidence on fish and sends strong scallops, Kyle redeems himself on meat, and the Red Team’s stations begin syncing up as well. That aligns with your looking-for signals (feel-good content, family-friendly, uplifting moments) and keeps your configured character spotlight visible, while steering clear of violence, harsh language, elimination drama.
recommended_rail.clip_label: Both kitchens recover and find rhythm
recommended_rail.clip_description: After the rough opening, the kitchens settle.  Egypt regains confidence on fish and sends strong scallops, Kyle redeems himself on meat, and the Red Team’s stations begin syncing up as well.

recommended_rail.title: Whit beats Kyle in the tiebreaker
recommended_rail.rationale: Feel-Good Family · Whit beats Kyle in the tiebreaker — The last matchup comes down to Whit versus Kyle on lamb
recommended_rail.audience_alignment_reasoning: For a Feel-Good Family viewer, "Whit beats Kyle in the tiebreaker" in Ep 5 delivers warmth and resolution without harsh conflict — ideal for co-viewing families. In this window, Gordon Ramsay, George Motz, Whit drive the beat: The last matchup comes down to Whit versus Kyle on lamb.  Whit’s burger is praised as intense and memorable, while Kyle’s burger gets good notes but cannot overcome the earlier mistake hanging over his overall performance. That aligns with your looking-for signals (feel-good content, family-friendly, uplifting moments) and keeps your configured character spotlight visible, while steering clear of violence, harsh language, elimination drama.

recommended_rail.title: Hannah seals the Red Team win
recommended_rail.rationale: Feel-Good Family · Hannah seals the Red Team win — With the score still in reach for both teams, the final pairing comes down to Shant and Hannah
recommended_rail.audience_alignment_reasoning: For a Feel-Good Family viewer, "Hannah seals the Red Team win" in Ep 1 delivers warmth and resolution without harsh conflict — ideal for co-viewing families. In this window, Gordon Ramsay, Hannah, Shant drive the beat: With the score still in reach for both teams, the final pairing comes down to Shant and Hannah.  Shant posts a respectable 4, but Hannah's maple leaf duck breast with curry chocolate demi-glace earns a 5 from Ramsay, flipping the ending into a Red Team victory and unleashing the women's celebration. That aligns with your looking-for signals (feel-good content, family-friendly, uplifting moments) and keeps your configured character spotlight visible, while steering clear of violence, harsh language, elimination drama.

Shant posts a respectable 4, but Hannah's maple leaf duck breast with curry chocolate demi-glace earns a 5 from Ramsay, flipping the ending into a Red Team victory and unleashing the women's celebration.

recommended_rail.title: Blue Team is thrown out after the second raw lamb
recommended_rail.rationale: Feel-Good Family · Blue Team is thrown out after the second raw lamb — After the Blue Team serves Vernon Davis a second raw lamb, Gordon Ramsay finally loses patience and marches the entire team into the pantry
recommended_rail.audience_alignment_reasoning: For a Feel-Good Family viewer, "Blue Team is thrown out after the second raw lamb" in Ep 4 delivers warmth and resolution without harsh conflict — ideal for co-viewing families. In this window, Gordon Ramsay, Joe, Lulu drive the beat: After the Blue Team serves Vernon Davis a second raw lamb, Gordon Ramsay finally loses patience and marches the entire team into the pantry.  He tears into them for showing no bounce-back ability and then throws them out of service, forcing them to leave the kitchen in disgrace. That aligns with your looking-for signals (feel-good content, family-friendly, uplifting moments) and keeps your configured character spotlight visible, while steering clear of violence, harsh language, elimination drama.

recommended_rail.title: Blue-team communication cracks under entrée pressure
recommended_rail.rationale: Feel-Good Family · Blue-team communication cracks under entrée pressure — The blue team’s first major collapse comes on appetizers and early entrees: scallops are overcooked, then salmon is overcooked and steak undercooked, while Egypt’s aggressive leadership starts provoking Michael, Kyle, and Brandon
recommended_rail.audience_alignment_reasoning: For a Feel-Good Family viewer, "Blue-team communication cracks under entrée pressure" in Ep 3 delivers warmth and resolution without harsh conflict — ideal for co-viewing families. In this window, Gordon Ramsay, Egypt, Corbin drive the beat: The blue team’s first major collapse comes on appetizers and early entrees: scallops are overcooked, then salmon is overcooked and steak undercooked, while Egypt’s aggressive leadership starts provoking Michael, Kyle, and Brandon.  On the red side, Hannah forgets the lentils for salmon, drawing Ramsay’s anger, but the women regain control faster than the men. That aligns with your looking-for signals (feel-good content, family-friendly, uplifting moments) and keeps your configured character spotlight visible, while steering clear of violence, harsh language, elimination drama.
recommended_rail.clip_label: Blue-team communication cracks under entrée pressure
recommended_rail.clip_description: The blue team’s first major collapse comes on appetizers and early entrees: scallops are overcooked, then salmon is overcooked and steak undercooked, while Egypt’s aggressive leadership starts provoking Michael, Kyle, and Brandon.  On the red side, Hannah forgets the lentils for salmon, drawing Ramsay’s anger, but the women regain control faster than the men.
recommended_rail.sub_clip_focus: Blue-team communication cracks under entrée pressure — The blue team’s first major collapse comes on appetizers and early entrees: scallops are overcooked, then salmon is overcooked and steak undercooked, while Egypt’s aggressive leadership starts provoking Michael, Kyle, and Brandon

recommended_rail.title: Farm punishment turns into a health scare
recommended_rail.rationale: Feel-Good Family · Farm punishment turns into a health scare — The losing Red Team is sent to a farm punishment involving manure, and the physical toll quickly shows
recommended_rail.audience_alignment_reasoning: For a Feel-Good Family viewer, "Farm punishment turns into a health scare" in Ep 4 delivers warmth and resolution without harsh conflict — ideal for co-viewing families. In this window, Amanda, Brittany drive the beat: The losing Red Team is sent to a farm punishment involving manure, and the physical toll quickly shows.  Amanda becomes overwhelmed and nearly collapses, while Brittany stays with her, coaches her breathing, and helps steady her until she recovers. That aligns with your looking-for signals (feel-good content, family-friendly, uplifting moments) and keeps your configured character spotlight visible, while steering clear of violence, harsh language, elimination drama.

resolved_clips.description: The blue team’s first major collapse comes on appetizers and early entrees: scallops are overcooked, then salmon is overcooked and steak undercooked, while Egypt’s aggressive leadership starts provoking Michael, Kyle, and Brandon.  On the red side, Hannah forgets the lentils for salmon, drawing Ramsay’s anger, but the women regain control faster than the men.

resolved_clips.title: Farm punishment turns into a health scare
resolved_clips.sceneTitle: Farm punishment turns into a health scare
resolved_clips.description: The losing Red Team is sent to a farm punishment involving manure, and the physical toll quickly shows.  Amanda becomes overwhelmed and nearly collapses, while Brittany stays with her, coaches her breathing, and helps steady her until she recovers.
